summary: The main claim is: But I think this area is severely under-researched, and we need to start rethinking the entire nature of the game.
If we can’t find a winning strategy with the current game, perhaps we need to devote our attention toward a superior game board.
The main claim is: As AI systems get more capable, they may at some point be able to help us with alignment research.
I’d be interested in more research on what this would suggest about alignment automation.
The main claim is: GPT-5 in fact was let-down compared to the hopes and fears of people.
Every n weeks, twice as many people are thinking and talking about AI until it's most of the world.

The main claim is: And even if those arcane results are where the real x-risk lies, it’s easier to build an intuition for the risks by working from the bottom-up, as scenarios in which “AGI gets an upgrade and escapes the lab” require a conceptual leap that’…
The current trend is for these models to become smaller and smaller to the point where they will soon run locally on a smartphone.
The main claim is: Therefore, in this pretraining phase, I do put the agent in a situation where it has the ability to put in earplugs.
For example: + What happens if the agent always has the ability to put in earplugs, also during RL training?
The main claim is: I think it's pretty important to think about what exactly is in the training data, rather than about some general idea of accurately simulating the world.

I think there are two issues here: Thinking about it as 'you prompt it with some setting in the world, then it predicts this perfectly' + There's not a well-defined correct generalization unless this exact sequence of tokens was actually i…
The main claim is: Earlier this year I offered to bet people who had short AI timelines.
Even after adjusting for the bandwagon effect, I think it's now appropriate to update substantially as well.
The main claim is: In "No Press Diplomacy", players can't communicate.
--------------------- CICERO has two components: a strategy engine and a dialogue engine.
The main claim is: Box B contains $1,000,000 if Omega predicted that your auction will be won by economist Paul Milgrom.
Omega predicted you would do this, but Omega didn't want to believe it.

The main claim is: One reason I think I have for this intuition comes from extending an occasionally-repeated idea about human intelligence: that humans are nearly the dumbest possible creature capable of developing a technological civilisation.
The main claim is: Transformative AI will revolutionize the ways we interact with the world.

But maybe narrow tool AIs are already starting to do so: Every week, there are new startups, websites, and tools launched around using modern machine learning techniques to solve everyday problems, and we might as well use them to amplify…
